danger_signals.visual_form: Animated guard character moving on screen
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat to player; direct contact causes loss of life

danger_signals.visual_form: Guard sprite with gold bar visible
danger_signals.meaning: Guard has collected loot; player must intercept or trap to recover
Imminent danger if player is in hole; will result in death if not escaped

danger_signals.visual_form: Guard sprite ascending/descending same ladder as player
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent collision threat; guard may move unpredictably
Guard is falling; player falls faster and can use this as tactical opportunity or hazard

danger_signals.visual_form: Player character in dug hole with no visible escape route
danger_signals.meaning: Level failure state; only escape is abort (costs life)

safety_signals.visual_form: Gold piece disappears from level; progress indicator updates
safety_signals.meaning: Objective progress; player moving toward level completion

safety_signals.visual_form: Guard sprite stuck in dug floor, unable to climb out
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary safety; guard neutralized as immediate threat

safety_signals.visual_form: Player on high ladder or platform with distance to nearest guard
safety_signals.meaning: Spatial safety; time to plan next move

safety_signals.signal: Top of screen reached with all gold collected
safety_signals.visual_form: Player reaching top boundary after final gold acquisition
safety_signals.meaning: Level complete; advancement and extra life awarded

safety_signals.signal: Guard respawning at top after hole fills
safety_signals.visual_form: Guard appearing at level apex in respawn state
safety_signals.meaning: Threat repositioned; player has time window before guard descends

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Can dig holes; moves faster than guards; leaves behind dug floors as tactical terrain

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Distinct character sprite differentiated from player
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: All guards share identical visual appearance; no faction differentiation among guards
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Can carry gold; confined to platform movement; respawn at level top when trapped

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Gold bar sprites distributed across level
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Multiple placement points; can be held by guards or player; determines level completion state

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Gold bars distributed across multi-story platform layout; guards positioned at top of level; player at starting position
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Guards begin patrolling and moving toward player according to AI logic; player has initial window to plan strategy

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fewer gold pieces visible on screen; some positions now empty
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Guard behavior adapts based on their pathing logic; guards may move toward uncollected gold or toward player

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Gold bar sprite attached to or visible on guard character
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Guard continues movement pattern; if trapped in hole, gold drops and becomes available for player pickup

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Floor platform shows visual gap/hole; brick visuals replaced by empty space
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Guards and player fall through if passing over; hole gradually fills, closing the gap over time

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Guard sprite contained within dug pit; unable to climb out visually
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Guard immobilized; will be consumed and respawn at level top if hole fills before escape

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: No gold bars remain on screen; level objective completed visually
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must navigate to top of screen to finish level; guards continue active pursuit

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player sprite at top boundary of level
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Level transitions to next stage; life counter increments; new level layout appears

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Guard and player sprites occupy same position
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player sprite disappears; life counter decrements; level restarts from beginning

environmental_storytelling.example: Level layout with ladders and hand-to-hand bars creates implicit narrative of industrial mining structure; vertical arrangement suggests descent into mines
Placement of gold bars throughout level creates implicit pathfinding narrative; player must decode intended route through spatial puzzle

environmental_storytelling.technique: Guard respawn point at level top
environmental_storytelling.example: Guards always reappear at top of screen after being consumed, suggesting a hierarchical threat model and unlimited enemy supply
Mixed floor types (normal, trapdoor, bedrock) create implicit environmental hazards and restrictions; bedrock marks impassable boundaries
Mechanical constraint creates implicit narrative that guards are less agile than player; elevates player skill advantage in puzzle solution

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can move left/right, climb ladders, hang from bars, fall from any height, dig holes in adjacent floors
Trapped; cannot climb out; death if hole fills before escape via lateral movement; can be liberated if guards dig from adjacent side
Can safely walk over trapped guards; permitted contact from above; must avoid initiating downward movement or contact becomes fatal
Moves along platforms in alternating pattern; does not always take shortest path to player; sometimes appears repelled depending on relative positioning
Pursues player across platforms and ladders; can climb and traverse level layout; if player and guard on same ladder, guard may move away counterintuitively
Can carry max one gold bar; if trapped in hole during carry, drops gold; if player is on guard's head while digging, player must move opposite direction when guard begins escape
Immobilized; cannot climb out; respawns at level top if hole fills completely; can be liberated if player digs while standing on guard's head
Falls faster than visual suggests; player falls faster than guards; guards can be used as falling bridges to reach otherwise unreachable areas
Immediately respawns at random location at level top; creates cyclical threat reset
Can be collected by player or guards; guards picking up gold restrict their carrying capacity and mobility options
Supports player and guard movement; can be dug by player to create holes
Cannot be dug; acts as impassable barrier; forces alternative pathfinding routes
Player and guards fall through if stepped on; creates hazard and alternate pathfinding option
Traps guards and player; allows passage of falling entities; gradually fills over time
Visual closure animation; player and guards must escape before complete closure or face consumption

notes: Lode Runner uses minimal visual differentiation between player and guards, relying instead on behavioral tells and spatial positioning to communicate threat and safety. The game's danger signals are primarily behavioral (guard movement patterns) and mechanical (hole filling timers) rather than color-coded or symbolic. World state is communicated through platform configuration changes, entity position, and visual disappearance/appearance of objectives. No faction-based visual identity exists beyond player vs. guard distinction. Environmental storytelling is implicit through mechanical constraints and level architecture rather than explicit narrative.
